purpose: Uses shale breakeven economics to define a vol-attractor band for oil prices that creates within-band self-regulation and outside-band amplification
components: Within-band: self-regulating production response dampens price vol
Outside-band: positive feedback amplifies price movements
Shale treadmill effect: potential narrowing of the attractor band over time
current_assessment: The mechanism is intuitive and grounded in well-documented shale economics, but not corroborated by other agents. The specific breakeven ranges are subject to service cost inflation/deflation. The claim that the band may be narrowing is unvalidated.
limitations: Breakeven ranges shift with service costs
Interaction with OPEC+ policy not modeled
Single-agent analysis with no debate challenge
